page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7ne.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores using an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor includes a 33 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced vector extensions including AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions (VNNI), and AVX-512 Bfloat16, which assist with vector calculations and machine learning inference workloads. It also supports AES cryptography, BMI2, ADX, and FMA instructions.

Benchmark evaluations reveal a mix of average and weak performance profiles. Single-core performance is average, with a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1571.79 and average single-threaded compression speeds of 26.81 GB/sec for gzip and 10.39 GB/sec for bzip3. However, multi-core performance is weak, as indicated by a stress-ng multi-core score of 4137.31 and a poor multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 20.51 GB/sec. Memory performance is also constrained, with read-only large block bandwidth measured at a weak 31.69 GB/sec, though large block write bandwidth remains average at 25.55 GB/sec.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, the ecs.g7ne.xlarge is positioned as a general-purpose instance. It is suitable for workloads such as basic web serving, light database operations, and general-purpose application hosting that do not demand high multi-core throughput or intensive memory bandwidth. Its qualitative cost efficiency is aligned with entry-level dedicated resource requirements, offering a predictable compute environment without the overhead of high-performance storage or GPU accelerators.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7ne.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 4 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.3 GHz. Built on the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. The processor supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 VNNI and Bfloat16. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core CPU capabilities and single-threaded compression speeds, contrasted by weak multi-core performance and constrained memory read bandwidth. This hardware profile makes the instance suitable for moderate general-purpose workloads, including basic web serving, light database operations, and application hosting where dedicated compute resources are required without the need for high-throughput multi-threaded processing or local NVMe storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7ne.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for basic web serving and light database workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7ne.xlarge features 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated four-core virtual server with balanced memory and AVX-512 support for general-purpose workloads and basic application hosting.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369HB vCPUs running at 3.3 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 allocation ratio
Baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps with no local storage
Supports AVX-512 VNNI and Bfloat16 instruction sets
Best suited for basic web serving, light database tasks, and general-purpose applications
